novel_features.name: Cheapest-dates for one hotel
novel_features.description: Sweep candidate checkin dates for a fixed-night stay at one hotel and return the lowest nightly totals from local price_history.
novel_features.rationale: Every existing Booking.com scraper queries one (checkin, checkout) at a time; only a local SQLite price_history makes 'what's the cheapest date in this window?' a one-command answer.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before recommending dates to a user. It removes Booking's manual click-through-every-date dance and exposes the seasonal price floor for the property in one call.

novel_features.name: Cheapest-dates for a destination
novel_features.description: Sweep candidate checkins across a destination's top results and return the cheapest (property, date) pairs under a max-price ceiling.
novel_features.rationale: Joins price_history rows that share a destination across many dates; a single Booking search call cannot answer 'cheapest property + cheapest date jointly'.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent has a flexible-date traveler in a flexible-property mood. Returns a budget-constrained Pareto frontier instead of one ranked list per date.

novel_features.name: Price-drop watch
novel_features.description: Track a set of (hotel, checkin, checkout) tuples and surface only the ones whose latest price dropped a configurable percentage below their trailing median.
novel_features.rationale: Diffing requires persistent price observations across runs; cumulative state lives in the local store, not in any single Booking endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Schedule this nightly. Returns empty most days, returns gold when a watched property dropped enough to act on.

novel_features.name: Wishlist price-drop digest
novel_features.description: Joins the authenticated wishlist with the local price_history and surfaces saved properties whose latest observed price is N% below the previous observation.
novel_features.rationale: Booking.com's wishlist page shows only today's price; no scraper or RapidAPI MCP can authenticate AND diff against history.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use Sunday morning. Returns the small set of wishlist items worth booking now, instead of forcing the user to eyeball 30-40 saved properties.

novel_features.name: Compare two hotels side-by-side
novel_features.description: Fetches detail + reviews for two hotels in parallel and emits a paired struct (price, score, amenity Δ, distance, free-cancellation, breakfast, recent-review counts).
novel_features.rationale: Booking has no two-hotel comparison endpoint; the diff is local synthesis across two JSON-LD payloads + two review streams.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent has narrowed to two finalists, reach for this instead of re-rendering both detail pages and asking the user to read both.

novel_features.name: Free-cancellation deadline alarm
novel_features.description: Walks authenticated upcoming trips, extracts the free-cancellation-until deadline from each trip detail, and returns trips whose deadline is within a configurable window.
novel_features.rationale: Booking's free-cancellation policy is a service-specific identity feature; surfacing it in one command requires authenticated trip walks no scraper supports.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Booking penalizes missed cancellation deadlines. Run this each Monday morning to catch deadlines before they expire.

novel_features.name: Multi-leg itinerary planner
novel_features.description: Given multiple destination + date legs and a total budget, picks the cheapest property per leg whose summed nightly totals fit the budget, with a bounded combinatorial fallback when greedy busts.
novel_features.rationale: Booking's UI doesn't compose results across destinations; cross-leg optimization happens locally over multiple absorbed search calls.
novel_features.example: booking-com-pp-cli trip plan --leg Rome:2026-07-10:2026-07-13 --leg Florence:2026-07-13:2026-07-16 --leg Venice:2026-07-16:2026-07-20 --budget 1800 --filters free_cancellation,breakfast,score>=8 --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: For multi-city European itineraries the agent can answer the budget-constrained question in one round-trip instead of asking the user to iterate per leg.

novel_features.name: Genius-tier price-impact estimator
novel_features.description: Runs an absorbed search twice — once with the authenticated cookie (Genius rates applied) and once anonymously — and diffs price-per-property to surface the Genius savings delta.
novel_features.rationale: Booking.com hides whether a given search benefited from Genius until you compare; only a CLI that owns both cookie + cookieless surf calls can compute it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent helps a user evaluate whether a Booking Genius tier is worth chasing. Reports the actual unlocked discount on a real search.

novel_features.name: Mobile-only rate detector
novel_features.description: Re-runs an absorbed search with a Chrome mobile UA on top of the desktop call and diffs to surface mobile-only discounts Booking hides from desktop users.
novel_features.rationale: Booking's mobile-only rates are a documented identity feature; no scraper exploits the desktop/mobile pricing gap because they only ship one UA.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent is hunting savings, reach for this before recommending the desktop-quoted rate. Mobile rates can be 5-15 percent lower on the same hotel + dates.

novel_features.name: Destination price-band by month
novel_features.description: Aggregates price_history for a destination's synced properties and emits per-month median, min, and max nightly rate plus contributing property-count.
novel_features.rationale: Booking has no public seasonality endpoint; the per-month waterfall is a local aggregation only possible after `sync` builds the price_history backlog.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when planning a flexible-month trip. Returns the cheapest month + 'avoid this month' signal without manual searching.

novel_features.name: Trips export for expense systems
novel_features.description: Walks authenticated past-trip list, opens each trip detail, and emits a deterministic CSV (confirmation, property, checkin, checkout, currency, total, address) ready to paste into expense systems.
novel_features.rationale: Booking's web UI shows trips one-by-one; no scraper supports authenticated trip detail. Structured CSV is the agent-native shape for downstream automation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use Monday morning for last week's reimbursements. One call replaces clicking through every past trip.

novel_features.name: Offline property FTS
novel_features.description: After `sync` populates the local property store, FTS5 ranks free-text queries over name + description + amenity strings with BM25, no network call.
novel_features.rationale: Booking's hotel-name search is destination-locked; an offline FTS lets agents query 'boutique near louvre with rooftop' across already-synced properties.
novel_features.example: booking-com-pp-cli search "boutique near louvre with rooftop" --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: After repeated `sync` calls have built a corpus, this lets an agent answer cross-destination property questions without re-hitting the network.

novel_features.name: Review stats rollup
novel_features.description: Local SQL group-by over synced reviews; counts and median score per score-band, language, and traveler-type bucket. Mechanical, no NLP.
novel_features.rationale: Booking's review API returns flat lists; aggregating sentiment buckets requires local rollup, the only way to answer 'is this hotel good for solo travelers?' without reading every review.
novel_features.example: booking-com-pp-cli reviews stats --slug auliviaopera --country fr --by score-band,language,traveler-type --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent is matching property fit to a traveler type. Returns a bucket distribution instead of forcing the agent to read 1000 reviews.

narrative.headline: Every Booking.com workflow, plus offline price history, wishlist drop alerts, and multi-leg planning no other Booking.com tool ships.
narrative.value_prop: Search Booking.com, scrape hotel detail and reviews, watch prices over time, and read your trips, wishlist, and Genius rewards via a Chrome cookie import. Local SQLite price_history powers cheapest-date sweeps, price-drop watch, wishlist diff, and seasonal price-band rollups that the booking.com UI cannot answer.
narrative.auth_narrative: Public search, hotel detail, reviews, destinations, and map markers run anonymously through Surf with a Chrome TLS fingerprint that clears Booking.com's AWS WAF challenge without a clearance cookie. Authenticated commands (trips, wishlist, rewards, profile) need a logged-in Chrome session: run `booking-com-pp-cli auth login --chrome` once and the CLI imports the booking.com session cookies so subsequent authenticated calls replay through Surf.

quickstart.comment: Headline hotel search — 25 SSR-extracted property cards with --select narrowing the response shape.
Drill into a property: amenities, address, geo, rating from the JSON-LD Hotel schema.
Local price_history sweep — answers 'when is this hotel cheapest in summer?' in one call.
Verify auth + reachability before authenticated commands. Run `booking-com-pp-cli auth login --chrome` first if doctor reports missing cookies.
Weekly digest of wishlist items whose price dropped at least 5 percent in the last 7 days.
Monday-morning alarm: upcoming trips whose free-cancellation deadline is within 7 days.

troubleshoots.symptom: All search calls return HTTP 202 with 'Just a moment' HTML body
troubleshoots.fix: Booking.com refreshed its AWS WAF signature. Run `booking-com-pp-cli doctor` to confirm reachability; if the Surf+Chrome probe is failing, upgrade printing-press so the bundled Surf transport ships the latest Chrome TLS fingerprint.

troubleshoots.symptom: Authenticated commands (trips, wishlist, rewards) redirect to the sign-in page
troubleshoots.fix: Your imported cookies expired. Re-run `booking-com-pp-cli auth login --chrome` after refreshing the booking.com tab in Chrome.

troubleshoots.symptom: Map markers command returns 'CSRF token expired'
troubleshoots.fix: The internal /dml/graphql endpoint rotates the CSRF token every ~30 minutes. The CLI auto-refreshes by re-fetching the search-results page; pass `--no-cache` to force a fresh fetch on the next call.

troubleshoots.symptom: `prices cheapest` returns fewer rows than --window days
troubleshoots.fix: Booking shows 'sold out' for some dates and drops them from the SSR card list. Inspect with `--include-unavailable` to see which dates were probed without prices.

troubleshoots.symptom: `wishlist drops` returns empty even after observed price changes
troubleshoots.fix: Drop detection requires >= 2 observations per item. Run `booking-com-pp-cli wishlist get` daily (or wire it to a cron) before `wishlist drops` will diff meaningfully.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent needs to search Booking.com, drill into hotel detail, compare candidates, or work with a user's logged-in Booking account (trips, wishlist, Genius rewards). It is the right choice for cheapest-date sweeps, price-drop tracking, and multi-leg itinerary planning — work that compounds across calls and benefits from local SQLite history. Reach for it instead of generic web-scraping tools because it owns Booking's AWS WAF clearance via Surf+Chrome TLS, the CSRF/cookie composition for /dml/graphql, and the Chrome cookie import for authenticated surfaces.

recipes.title: Cheapest week in Paris this summer
recipes.explanation: Sweep every 7-night window in summer and return the cheapest property-and-date pairs under $200/night, with --select narrowing the response to the three fields an agent needs.

recipes.title: Wishlist drop digest for Sunday morning
recipes.explanation: Walks the user's wishlist and returns only items whose latest price is at least 5 percent below the previous observation in the last week.

recipes.title: Compare two hotels with detailed deltas
recipes.explanation: Side-by-side amenities, price, score, distance, free-cancellation, and recent-review counts for two slug-identified properties.

recipes.title: Free-cancellation alarm for the week ahead
recipes.explanation: Walks authenticated upcoming trips and surfaces those with a free-cancellation deadline in the next week, sorted by urgency.

recipes.title: Plan a 3-city Italian itinerary against a budget
recipes.command: booking-com-pp-cli trip plan --leg Rome:2026-07-10:2026-07-13 --leg Florence:2026-07-13:2026-07-16 --leg Venice:2026-07-16:2026-07-20 --budget 1800 --filters free_cancellation,breakfast,score>=8 --agent
recipes.explanation: Searches each leg in parallel and picks the cheapest property combination per leg whose summed nightly totals fit the budget.

trigger_phrases: what's the cheapest week to stay in
track price drops on my booking.com wishlist
are any of my booking trips about to lose free cancellation
plan a multi-city trip on booking.com
